content: Run `bun run lint:fix` before `bun run lint` to auto-fix biome's organize-imports + format issues. Biome treats sort order on named re-exports and minor formatting (e.g. wrapping a const initializer that fits on one line) as errors when invoked with `--error-on-warnings`, so manual edits will fail CI even when the code is otherwise correct.

.devcontainer/ image must satisfy two contracts simultaneously: (1) every binary declared in burrow.toml's [toolchain] block (bw doctor / bw up gate on PATH availability inside the canonical Linux dev env, mx-52752e), and (2) every CLI CLAUDE.md mandates for session rituals (ml prime, sd prime, sd close, sd sync). Adding a new [toolchain] entry or a new mandatory CLI to CLAUDE.md without updating .devcontainer/Dockerfile makes fresh-container dogfood broken on first run. Concrete pins as of 2026-05-08: NodeSource for Node 20 (Ubuntu 24.04 ships 18); bun + @anthropic-ai/claude-code + @os-eco/mulch-cli@0.8.0 + @os-eco/seeds-cli@0.4.0 via 'bun install -g'. .devcontainer/devcontainer.json's postCreateCommand chains 'bun link' after 'bun install' so package.json's bin entries (bw, burrow) land on PATH globally inside the container.

Golden files for wire-shape lock tests (e.g. src/server/openapi/__golden__/openapi.json) live under a __golden__/ folder and are excluded from biome via files.includes: '!**/__golden__'. Biome's formatter would otherwise reformat the JSON's indentation/key-ordering on bun run lint and break the byte-exact lock test. The exclude pattern intentionally OMITS the trailing /** segment — biome 2.2+ flags '!**/__golden__/**' as useBiomeIgnoreFolder and rejects it.

When regenerating src/server/openapi/__golden__/openapi.json from spec.ts, write via process.stdout.write(serializeOpenApiDocument(doc)) — NOT console.log. serializeOpenApiDocument already terminates with a newline; console.log appends a second \n that breaks the byte-for-byte golden lock.

description: ml sync commits whatever is currently in the git index, not just .mulch/ paths — running 'git add src/foo.ts' then 'ml record ...' then 'ml sync' produces a single commit titled 'mulch: update expertise' that contains both the .mulch/ records AND the unrelated staged code. Mixes concerns and misleads history readers.
resolution: Before 'ml sync', either (a) commit non-mulch staged files separately first, or (b) 'git reset' to unstage non-mulch paths and let ml sync stage .mulch/ alone. Same caveat applies to 'sd sync' for non-seeds paths.
